content.problemStatement:
Sereja has an array **A** that contains **n** integers: **A1, A2, ..., An**. 
Sereja also has an array **B** that contains **n** integers **B1, B2, ..., Bn**. 
In a single step Sereja can choose two indexes **i** and **j** **(1 <= i <= j <= n)** , and increase all the elements of **A** with indices between **i** and **j** inclusively by one, modulo 4.
In other words, we make **Ap** equal to **(Ap + 1) modulo 4** if **p** is an integer from the range [**i**; **j**].

Now Sereja is interested in the following question: what is the mininal number of steps necessary in order to make the array **A** equal to the array **B**.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains three lines: the first line contains an integer n, the number of elements in the arrays A and B. The second line contains n space-separated integers representing the elements of array A. The third line contains n space-separated integers representing the elements of array B.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the minimum number of steps required to make array A equal to array B.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The minimum number of steps required to make array A equal to array B is 1. We can add 1 to the first element of A to make it equal to the first element of B, and then add 1 to the last element of A to make it equal to the last element of B.

The minimum number of steps required to make array A equal to array B is 2. We can add 3 to the first element of A to make it equal to the first element of B, and then add 1 to the last element of A to make it equal to the last element of B.

The minimum number of steps required to make array A equal to array B is 1. We can add 3 to the first element of A to make it equal to the first element of B, and then add 1 to the last element of A to make it equal to the last element of B.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using dynamic programming. We create a 2D array dp where dp[i][j] represents the minimum number of steps required to make the first i elements of array A equal to the first i elements of array B, with the last element being increased by j modulo 4. We fill up the dp array by iterating over the elements of the arrays and considering all possible increases for each element.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * n * 4) per test case, O(T * n * 4) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n * 4) — the dominant structure is the dp array.
